ogTitle: Pickanjinnie vs Picnic Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pickanjinnie and Picnic Bay.
heading: Pickanjinnie vs Picnic Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pickanjinnie, Queensland and Picnic Bay, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Picnic Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Pickanjinnie (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pickanjinnie or Picnic Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pickanjinnie has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Picnic Bay scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pickanjinnie scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Picnic Bay scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pickanjinnie or Picnic Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Picnic Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
